name: Keep the 128x128/128-thread hot-band branch but revert only the K32 mainloop back to proven K16 staging to localize correctness

summary.name: Keep the 128x128/128-thread hot-band branch but revert only the K32 mainloop back to proven K16 staging to localize correctness
summary.hypothesis: Round 14 finally found a high-ceiling family: the new 128x128x32 hot-band kernel cut hot-band time from about 41.62 ms to 34.98 ms and raised tensor active from about 38.13% to 45.21% and DRAM throughput from about 17.00% to 39.65%. That is too large a gain to throw away. The immediate problem is correctness, not throughput. Because the CTA shape, block size, and epilogue all changed together with the K32 stage logic, the fastest way to localize the bug is to keep the 128x128 CTA and 128-thread launch but revert only the hot-band mainloop to the simpler K16 double-buffer schedule. If that passes correctness, the error is in the K32 half-stage addressing / refill logic rather than in the new CTA shape itself.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Correctness bug likely in the K32 staged mainloop bookkeeping, not in the new 128x128 warp mapping.
summary.risk: Moderate. This keeps the promising new launch shape but should reduce logic complexity materially; the main risk is that performance drops enough to hide the upside of the new CTA structure.
summary.stop_condition: Reject this localization branch only if correctness still fails; otherwise keep the 128x128 family alive even if runtime regresses modestly.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
